title: Caloric Deficit Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate a healthy caloric deficit for sustainable weight loss. Find your daily calorie reduction to reach your goal weight safely.
intro: Reference values in health come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO). They provide a framework to interpret your results, but your personal health context always comes first. A healthy caloric deficit helps you lose weight sustainably. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **500 kcal = 0.5 kg/week**. Apply directly with your inputs. A 500-calorie daily deficit creates sustainable, healthy weight loss.
useCases: Technical calculations for healthy caloric deficits
explanation:
**Healthy reference values** come from large population studies (NHANES, WHO, CDC). They vary by age, sex, ethnicity, and region. These are guidelines; your personal health history always takes precedence.

## How It's Calculated

A healthy caloric deficit supports sustainable weight loss.

This calculator applies the formula using your inputs, checking for reasonable ranges and alerting you to values outside the typical zone.

## Common Health Reference Ranges

| Parameter | Normal adult range |
|---|---|
| Blood pressure | <120/80 mmHg |
| Resting heart rate | 60–100 bpm |
| Body temperature | 36.1–37.2°C |
| Oxygen saturation (SpO2) | 95–100% |
| Fasting blood glucose | 70–100 mg/dL |
| HbA1c | <5.7% |
| Total cholesterol | <200 mg/dL |
| LDL cholesterol | <100 mg/dL |
| HDL cholesterol | >40 (M) / >50 (F) |

## Final Notes

This calculation is a reference guide. For critical decisions (medical, financial, legal), consult with a qualified healthcare professional. Values are current through 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What's a healthy weekly weight loss rate?
faq.a: 0.5–1 kg per week (1–2 lbs). Women need at least 1,500 calories daily, men 1,800. Slower, steady loss is more sustainable.

faq.q: How do I calculate my caloric deficit?
faq.a: Determine your maintenance calories, then subtract 500–1,000 for a deficit that yields 0.5–1 kg weekly loss.

faq.q: How many calories equal 1 kg of body fat?
faq.a: Approximately 7,700 calories. A 500-calorie daily deficit creates roughly 0.5 kg (1 lb) of loss per week.

faq.q: Is rapid weight loss better than slow weight loss?
faq.a: No. Crash diets often cause metabolic adaptation and weight rebound. A moderate deficit is more sustainable.

faq.q: How does metabolic adaptation affect dieting?
faq.a: Extended deficits slow your metabolism and reduce energy expenditure. This is why very restrictive diets become less effective over time.

faq.q: How much protein do I need while dieting?
faq.a: Aim for 1.6–2.2 grams per kg of body weight to preserve muscle mass during weight loss.

faq.q: Should I combine cardio and strength training?
faq.a: Yes. Pairing cardio with resistance training optimizes fat loss while preserving muscle mass and body composition.

faq.q: When should I see a doctor about weight loss?
faq.a: Consult a healthcare professional if you have concerning symptoms, values outside normal ranges, or a family history of metabolic or heart disease.

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
